title: Sharadiya Navratri
intro: Sharadiya Navratri is one of the most sacred and widely celebrated Hindu festivals dedicated to the worship of the nine divine forms of Goddess Durga.
description:
Sharadiya Navratri is one of the most sacred and widely celebrated Hindu festivals dedicated to the worship of the nine divine forms of Goddess Durga. The festival is observed for nine days, beginning on the Pratipada (first day) of the Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Ashwin and ending on Navami. During this auspicious period, devotees observe fasts, perform Ghatasthapana (Kalash Sthapana), chant Durga Mantras, and offer daily prayers to Goddess Durga.

According to Hindu scriptures, Goddess Durga fought the demon Mahishasura for nine days and defeated him on the tenth day, symbolizing the victory of good over evil. Each day of Navratri is dedicated to one of the Navadurga forms, and devotees seek blessings for strength, wisdom, prosperity, and protection.

Sharadiya Navratri is celebrated with great devotion across India through Durga Puja, recitation of the Durga Saptashati, devotional songs, Garba and Dandiya dances, temple visits, and Kanya Pujan. The festival inspires faith, self-discipline, spiritual growth, and the triumph of righteousness.

Festival Month

Ashwin (September – October)

Significance / Importance

Sharadiya Navratri holds immense religious and spiritual significance in Hinduism. It is a festival dedicated to honoring the divine feminine energy (Shakti) represented by Goddess Durga. During these nine days, devotees pray for courage, health, wisdom, prosperity, and protection from negative influences.

Each day is devoted to one of the nine forms of Goddess Durga, known as Navadurga. Many devotees observe fasting, perform daily worship, chant sacred mantras, and read the Durga Saptashati to seek the Goddess’s blessings. Kanya Pujan, performed on Ashtami or Navami, is considered highly auspicious, as young girls are worshipped as manifestations of the Divine Mother.

The festival concludes with Vijayadashami (Dussehra), which celebrates the victory of righteousness over evil and marks the beginning of new opportunities and success.

Puja Vidhi

1. Perform Ghatasthapana (Kalash Sthapana) on the first day in the auspicious Muhurat.
2. Place an idol or image of Goddess Durga in the worship area.
3. Light an Akhand Jyoti (continuous sacred lamp).
4. Worship one form of Navadurga each day.
5. Chant Durga Mantras or recite the Durga Saptashati and Durga Chalisa.
6. Observe fasting with devotion according to tradition.
7. Perform Kanya Pujan on Ashtami or Navami.
8. Conclude the worship with Durga Aarti and prayers for peace, prosperity, and divine blessings.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Navratri Vrat Katha
* Durga Saptashati
* Durga Mantra
* Durga Aarti
* Durga Chalisa
* Navadurga (Nine Forms of Goddess Durga)

faq.a1: Sharadiya Navratri is celebrated from Pratipada to Navami during the Shukla Paksha of the Ashwin month.
faq.a2: It commemorates Goddess Durga’s victory over the demon Mahishasura and symbolizes the triumph of good over evil.
faq.q3: Which Goddess is worshipped during Navratri?
faq.a3: The nine forms of Goddess Durga, collectively known as Navadurga, are worshipped during Navratri.
faq.q4: In which month is Sharadiya Navratri celebrated?
faq.a4: Sharadiya Navratri is usually observed in September or October.
faq.a5: Ghatasthapana, also known as Kalash Sthapana, is the sacred ritual that marks the beginning of Navratri.
faq.q6: Why is Kanya Pujan performed during Navratri?
faq.a6: Kanya Pujan honors young girls as the living embodiment of Goddess Durga and is considered highly auspicious.
faq.q7: Which scriptures are commonly recited during Navratri?
faq.a7: The Durga Saptashati, Durga Chalisa, and Durga Mantras are commonly recited during Navratri.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Sharadiya Navratri?
faq.a8: Sharadiya Navratri symbolizes devotion, inner strength, self-purification, and the victory of righteousness over evil.
